The '''Learning''' tab in '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Options]]''' provides options affecting the way you learn: 

[[Image:Options learning.jpg|center|800px|SuperMemo: Toolkit : Options : Learning tab]] 

Interpretation of individual fields: 

*'''<span id="Forgetting_index">Forgetting index</span>''' determines the default [[Glossary:Requested forgetting index|requested forgetting index]]. Forgetting index determines the speed of learning. When you first run SuperMemo, it is set to 10%. This means that SuperMemo will tune the learning process in such a way that you will stand a 10% chance of forgetting any given [[Glossary:Item|item]] at [[Glossary:Repetition|repetition]]. You can reduce the forgetting index to 3% at the cost of a significant slow-down of the learning process. You can also increase it up to 20% to greatly increase the [[Glossary:Acquisition rate|speed of learning]] at the cost of [[Glossary:Retention|knowledge retention]]. Increasing the forgetting index further makes little sense as both retention and the speed of learning will decrease (you can read about it in [http://super-memory.com/articles/theory.htm Theoretical aspects of SuperMemo] or see it for yourself by using '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Toolkit menu#Statistics|Statistics]] : Simulation'''). All individual [[Glossary:Item|items]] can have their individual forgetting index set with '''[[Element menu#Edit|Edit]] : [[Element parameters|Edit parameters]]''' on the [[Element menu|element menu]] (<code>Ctrl+Shift+P</code>). Read more: [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Forgetting_index_in_SuperMemo Forgetting index]
*'''Always randomize final drill''' - scramble the sequence of [[Glossary:Repetition|repetitions]] in the [[Glossary:Final drill|final drill]] before moving to the final drill stage 
*'''<span id="Skip_final_drill">Skip final drill</span>''' - do not create the [[Glossary:Final drill queue|final drill queue]]. [[Glossary:Final drill|Final drill]] must always be considered of lower priority than reviewing the [[Glossary:Outstanding material|outstanding material]]. For that reason, in a heavily overloaded incremental reading process you may come to a point where your learning sessions always end with '''[[Postpone]]''' (or start with '''[[Glossary:Auto-postpone|Auto-postpone]]'''). If this is the case, building the [[Glossary:Final drill queue|final drill queue]] makes little sense 
*'''<span id="Midnight_clock_shift">Midnight shift</span>''' - the number of hours after midnight on which the new repetition day begins. For your convenience you should put the midnight-clock-shift value to the middle of your average night. For example, if your average sleeping hours are 23:00 - 7:00 (i.e. 11 p.m. - 7 a.m.), the middle of your night is 3 a.m. If you then set your midnight shift at 3 a.m. (slider at 3), you are least likely to be in a situation when you need to change the midnight clock shift to correctly execute [[Glossary:Repetition|repetitions]] belonging to a given waking day. If on occasion you come back home late at 2:30 a.m. and are still able to make [[Glossary:Repetition|repetitions]], SuperMemo will behave like there was no date change at midnight. On the other hand, if you wake up at 3:30 a.m. and decide to make [[Glossary:Repetition|repetitions]] at that time, SuperMemo will already consider those [[Glossary:Repetition|repetitions]] as belonging to the new day. Midnight clock shift is extended to 16 hours for people in shift work and those who work in unusual hours (e.g. as a result of [http://super-memory.com/articles/sleep.htm DSPS or ASPS sleep disorders]). The change of date will not occur until you restart SuperMemo. For that reason, you should always close SuperMemo for the night.
*'''[[Algorithm Arena]] Alerts''' - Triggers warnings when the [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] calculated by Algorithm SM-20 significantly deviates from the value proposed by the legacy Algorithm SM-15.
**'''Difference [%]''' - Raises an alert when the [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] discrepancy (in percent) equals or exceeds this threshold.
**'''Interval [days]''' - Sets a minimum [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] length constraint. Discrepancies on intervals shorter than this value are ignored.
**'''Factor''' - Raises an alert when the [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] quotient compared to Algorithm SM-15 exceeds this limit.
**'''Strict check''' - Apply strict parameters for [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] comparisons. If different algorithms propose conflicting schedules, SuperMemo will frequently interrupt to let you manually select the [[Glossary:Optimum interval|optimum interval]].
**'''Relaxed check''' - Uses loose criteria so SuperMemo can automatically determine the [[Glossary:Optimum interval|optimum interval]] on its own. It does this by weighing the suggestions, heavily favoring the newer SM-19 and SM-20 algorithms.
**'''Default check''' - Sets a safe, balanced baseline for comparisons. You can relax these settings later once you are comfortable with the [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] weights SuperMemo picks for you.
